title: Four Against Darkness - Part 11 - Twisted Final Foes, Twisted Hordes, and Updated Buyer's Guide
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xe6w3VmUf5I

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into two powerhouse expansions—Twisted Final Fights and Twisted Hordes—while proposing a curated starter package for newcomers to Four Against Darkness. Twisted Final Fights stands out as potentially the best expansion in the entire product line, offering 48 fully-detailed boss encounters, each with custom art, special mechanics, and HCL (Hero Class Level) scaling. Every boss feels like a mini-game unto itself, with complications that go beyond simple dice rolls. The expansion also includes rules for hunting monsters across multiple missions and even building an enchanted homunculus character by collecting scattered pieces.

Twisted Hordes complements this with a massive D100 treasure table offering mundane, magical, and utterly bizarre items from explosive pumpkins to eternal bread baskets. Together, these expansions provide incredible depth and replay value. Daniel then recommends a dream starter package: core game plus Twisted Dungeons, Twisted Minions, Wayfarers and Adventurers, Fiendish Foes, Twisted Final Fights, and Twisted Hordes—roughly 60-80 dollars that would deliver dozens of hours of adventuring.

----

If you were building a Four Against Darkness collection from scratch, would you follow Daniel's suggested starter package, or would you prioritize different expansions based on what sounds most fun to you?
